stories.title: A Hezbollah commander describes battling Israel in Lebanon
stories.description: In a rare interview, a wounded Hezbollah commander tells NPR about his secretive Shia Muslim militia's new command structure and how it has managed to keep firing rockets into northern Israel.

stories.title: No Deal: U.S.-Iran peace talks in Islamabad collapse
stories.description: The United States and Iran failed to reach an agreement after a day of highly anticipated face-to-face peace talks, Washington's lead negotiator Vice President J.D. Vance announced on Sunday.

stories.title: Pakistan hosts historic face-to-face talks between the US and Iran
stories.description: In Pakistan's capital, Islamabad, officials from the US and Iran met to discuss a possible end to the war that's engulfed much of the Middle East.

stories.title: What's it like to cover the Ukraine war as the world's attention is focused on Iran?
stories.description: In this week's Reporter's Notebook conversation, we take a closer look at what it's like to cover the war in Ukraine, and how the war in Iran is changing that.

stories.title: Pope Leo says 'delusion of omnipotence' is fueling U.S.-Israeli war in Iran
stories.description: In the first weeks of the war, the Chicago-born Leo was initially reluctant to publicly condemn the violence and limited his comments to muted appeals for peace and dialogue. But Leo stepped up his criticism starting on Palm Sunday.

stories.title: We spoke to the man making viral Lego-style AI videos for Iran. Experts say it's powerful propaganda
stories.description: "Slopaganda" is too weak a term to capture how powerful this "highly sophisticated" content is, one expert says.

stories.title: Concerns after satellite provider restricts Iran images following US pressure
stories.description: Planet Labs says it is restricting coverage of Iran and parts of the Middle East indefinitely.

stories.title: Lebanon and Israel officials to meet in US on Tuesday
stories.description: A senior Lebanese official earlier told the BBC that Lebanon would only take part in talks if a ceasefire was in place.

stories.title: Historic Vance-Ghalibaf talks must bridge deep distrust
stories.description: Face-to-face talks would mark the highest-level engagement between Iran and the US since the 1979 Islamic Revolution.

stories.title: Trump says Iran's handling of Strait of Hormuz is 'not the agreement we have'
stories.description: The US president accused Iran of "doing a very poor job" with the waterway as Israel and Lebanon to begin peace talks.

stories.title: 'Endless fears': Even if fighting stops, the damage to Iran's children will endure
stories.description: The BBC has been able to obtain testimony from parents and those trying to help children deal with the distress that comes with war.

stories.title: Lebanon thought there was a ceasefire - then Israel unleashed deadly blitz
stories.description: Israel says Lebanon is not included in the ceasefire the US agreed to halt the war with Iran.

stories.title: BBC at the site of Israeli air strikes in Beirut
stories.description: At least 182 people were killed and 890 others wounded, according to the Lebanese health ministry.

stories.title: Has US achieved its war objectives in Iran?
stories.description: Key US objectives at the start of the war were to stop Iran getting a nuclear weapon and degrading its arsenal.

stories.title: Israeli strikes in Lebanon 'grave violation' of ceasefire, Iran minister tells BBC
stories.description: Iran's deputy foreign minister says the US must choose between 'war and ceasefire'.

stories.title: US and Iran end 21-hour ceasefire talks without agreement before Vance departs Pakistan
stories.description: The United States and Iran ended a historic round of face-to-face talks early Sunday without reaching an agreement and the fate of the fragile, two-week ceasefire still unclear. Vice President JD Vance, who led the US delegation during the 21 hours of talks in Pakistan's capital ...

stories.title: Iran-US: Talks ended after 21 hours without reaching agreement
stories.description: US Vice President JD Vance said on Sunday that 21 hours of peace talks in Pakistan between the US and Iran had failed to produce an agreement to end the war. Each side blamed the other for the failure of the negotiations.

stories.title: Middle East war live: US failed to gain Tehran's trust, Iranian negotiator says
stories.description: ⁠Iran's ⁠Parliamentary Speaker Mohammad ​Baqer Qalibaf ​said Sunday that Iran raised "forward-looking" ​initiatives ‌during talks ⁠in Islamabad ‌but that the US failed ⁠to "earn" the trust of the Iranian delegation. US Vice President JD Vance said earlier that 21 hours of peace t...

stories.title: Iran-US negotiations underway in Pakistan, US says Strait of Hormuz being 'cleared'
stories.description: Delegations from the United States and Iran held direct negotiations on Saturday for the first time since 1979 in Islamabad,Pakistan, to end a six-week conflict, while US President Donald Trump said that the US military had begun clearing mines from the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Iran: a mediator on the mediations
stories.description: Gavin Lee speaks with Oliver McTernan, Director of Forward Thinking, about the current situation in Iran, the Straight of Hormuz and Pakistan.

stories.title: 'The Last Cedar of Lebanon': A play exploring war’s lasting scars
stories.description: Aïda Asgharzadeh was widely praised for her powerful exploration of dissidence and exile in Persian Dolls, weaving her own family’s story into a drama spanning Iran and France. Her deft use of history as a backdrop to intimate, personal narratives is once again in focus with her ...

stories.title: Beirut airport remains operational despite Israeli bombings
stories.description: Despite Israeli airstrikes in the area, Beirut airport remains operational. Kept open through close coordination and international guarantees, it is operating at reduced capacity.

stories.title: What happened in 40 days of war on Iran
stories.description: Who got hit in the war on Iran? The attacks, the damage and the people caught in between.

stories.title: Iran war: What is happening on day 44 of the US-Iran conflict?
stories.description: US-Iran talks in Islamabad end without a deal, with each side blaming the other for the failure.

stories.title: Pakistan urges US and Iran to uphold ceasefire after talks end
stories.description: Pakistani Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar has insisted the US and Iran must uphold their ceasefire agreement.

stories.title: US and Iran fail to reach a deal after marathon talks in Pakistan
stories.description: US VP JD Vance says Iran chose not to accept US terms, while Iran says it did not expect a deal at the first meeting.

stories.title: Oil tankers exit Strait of Hormuz amid fragile US-Iran ceasefire
stories.description: Three supertankers laden with oil exit waterway amid global energy crunch, shipping data shows.

stories.title: Why did US-Iran talks end without an agreement in Pakistan?
stories.description: Despite high-level talks for the first time in years, the US and Iran couldn't reach agreement on terms to end the war.

stories.title: Watch JD Vance’s full remarks after US-Iran talks end without deal
stories.description: US Vice President JD Vance is leaving Pakistan, saying Tehran chose not to accept their ‘final and best offer’.

stories.title: US delegation leaves Pakistan without reaching Iran deal
stories.description: US Vice President JD Vance says the US delegation has failed to reach an agreement with Iran.

stories.title: Iran says time for US to decide ‘whether it can earn our trust’ after talks
stories.description: After their face-to-face talks yielded no agreement, Iran’s Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf on Sunday said it was now time for the United States to decide whether it can “earn” Tehran’s trust. In a post on X, written in Persian, Ghalibaf said: “America has understood ...

stories.title: Dar urges US, Iran to uphold commitment to ceasefire as talks conclude without deal
stories.description: Deputy Prime Minister and Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar urged the United States and Iran to remain committed to a ceasefire after both countries ended historic face-to-face talks without an agreement on Sunday. “It is imperative that the parties continue to uphold their commitment t...

stories.title: UN agency chiefs seek end to war rules violations in Middle East
stories.description: GENEVA: UN agency chiefs on Saturday demanded an end to impunity for widespread international law violations in the Middle East, as casualties pile up six weeks into the war unleashed by US-Israeli strikes on Iran. In a joint statement, the heads of multiple United Nations agenci...

stories.title: Pakistan sends military force, fighter jets to Saudi Arabia
stories.description: Pakistan has sent a military force and fighter jets to Saudi Arabia to boost security under a joint defence cooperation agreement between the two countries, the Saudi defence ministry said on Saturday. The Saudi ministry, in a statement, wrote that a military force and fighter je...

stories.title: Easter truce between Russia and Ukraine falters
stories.description: • Kyiv accuses Moscow of nearly 470 ceasefire violations• Both sides exchange 175 prisoners of war each KHARKIV: Ukraine’s military command accused Russia of repeatedly violating a truce to mark the Orthodox Easter Saturday with nearly 470 incidents ranging from air strikes and d...

stories.title: Israel kills 18 in Lebanon, West Bank and Gaza
stories.description: BEIRUT: Israeli strikes killed more than a dozen people in Lebanon and Gaza on Saturday, while an Israeli settler shot dead a Palestinian man in the occupied West Bank, according to officials. The widespread violence came a day after plans were announced for potential ceasefire t...

stories.title: Israel-Iran war LIVE updates: Iran says talks ended without deal due to 'excessive demands' made by U.S.
stories.description: Vance departs Pakistan after failing to reach deal with Iran

stories.title: Talks ended without deal due to 'excessive demands' made by U.S.: Iran
stories.description: Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesperson Esmaeil Baqaei, however, said Iran is determined to utilise all tools, including diplomacy, to secure national interests and protect the country's well-being

stories.title: Pakistan to continue facilitating U.S.-Iran talks, says Ishaq Dar; urges ceasefire
stories.description: The Pakistan-brokered talks, the first direct, high-level engagement between the two sides since the 1979 Islamic Revolution, were watched globally amid increasing expectations of a breakthrough.

stories.title: Vance leaves Pakistan after Iran talks fail to reach a deal
stories.description: No deal yet in US-Iran talks. Vice-President J.D. Vance says the U.S. is returning without an agreement after key differences over Iran’s nuclear programme. Iranian media says talks may continue, with the Strait of Hormuz at the centre of discussions.

stories.title: Islamabad talks: U.S. and Iran fail to reach agreement after 21 hours, Vance departs Pakistan
stories.description: Vance also praised Pakistan Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif and Chief of Army Staff Field Marshal Asim Munir, terming them “incredible hosts”

stories.title: Iran and Strait of Hormuz: A lone battle for control
stories.description: Historically, international straits have been flashpoints between littoral states and external powers, often serving as bargaining tools during conflicts
